query: SK Hynix ADR Nasdaq listing SKHY first trading date July 2026 latest news, pricing date, roadshow status, SEC review as of late June 2026. Is July 10 still the expected trading date? Any reports of delay or schedule slippage?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line:

**July 10, 2026 remains the expected trading date for SK Hynix's Nasdaq ADR debut (ticker SKHY), with no reports of delay or schedule slippage as of July 1, 2026.**

Key findings:
- **On track**: All sources from June 24 through July 1, 2026 consistently cite July 10 as the tentative debut date. The company actually *advanced* the process on June 30 by filing Amendment No. 1 to its Form F-1 — a normal forward step toward SEC effectiveness, not a setback.
- **SEC status**: Registration statement is not yet effective (standard "subject to completion" prospectus language). The SEC review is proceeding normally; earlier reporting had flagged expected clearance around the week of June 22. Company will list "as soon as practicable" after effectiveness.
- **Roadshow/pricing**: A global institutional roadshow (US/Europe/Asia) was planned; a non-deal roadshow and key preliminary steps were reportedly already completed by mid-June. Final price is set via book-building, priced a few trading days before the July 14 subscription date.
- **Offering**: Up to 17.79M ADSs, ~$29.4B target — potentially the largest ADR offering ever. Underwriters: BofA, Citi, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan.
- **Only caveat is routine**: All filings note the schedule is "tentative and subject to change," contingent on SEC effectiveness and market conditions. An earlier (June 10) "as early as August / ~$14B" rumor was superseded by the June 24 board resolution fixing the July 10 date and $29.4B size.

sources.snippet: Board resolution June 24, 2026; Nasdaq Global Select Market; scheduled listing July 10; new share listing July 29; subscription/payment July 14.

key_facts: SK Hynix's Nasdaq ADR listing (ticker SKHY) tentative first trading date remains July 10, 2026 as of July 1, 2026, with no reports of delay or schedule slippage.
Board approved the ADR issuance on June 24, 2026 (all six outside directors present); initial Form F-1 filed with SEC and securities registration filed with Korea Exchange same day.
On June 30, 2026, SK Hynix filed Amendment No. 1 to Form F-1 on SEC EDGAR, finalizing SKHY ticker and Nasdaq Global Select Market venue — a normal forward step, not a setback.
SEC registration statement had NOT yet become effective as of June 30; prospectus carries standard 'subject to completion' language; company will list 'as soon as practicable' after effectiveness.
Offering: up to 17.79 million new ADSs (~2.5% of ~712.7M shares), targeting ~45.45 trillion KRW (~$29.4B), potentially the largest ADR offering ever (surpassing Alibaba's $21.8B 2014 debut).
Estimated ADS price ~$166/unit; underwriters/global coordinators: BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan; depositary Citibank; 90-day lock-up.
Global institutional roadshow (US/Europe/Asia) planned; earlier reports (June 16) indicated a non-deal roadshow and key preliminary steps already completed; final price set via book-building.
Related key dates: subscription/payment July 14; new-share KRX listing and Q2 2026 earnings July 29, 2026.
Earlier June 10 report of 'as early as August, ~$14B' scenario was superseded by the June 24 board resolution fixing July 10 and $29.4B size.
Schedule remains 'tentative and subject to change' per all filings, contingent on SEC effectiveness, Korean/US regulatory approvals, and market conditions.
Offering sized so largest shareholder SK Square retains at least 20% stake, per South Korean regulatory requirement.
Minor risk factors (June 25 DRAM price-fixing class action, US-China supply chain scrutiny, AI capex slowdown) have not been reported to affect the listing timeline.
